zh.lifeSummary: 當Nicholas Le Gris於1442年出生在英國諾福爾可布洛迪申時，他的父親William Le Grys24歲，母親Margaret Hales20歲。他於1465年與Elizabeth Lapadge在英國英格蘭諾福爾可諾維其結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1467年在英國諾福爾可諾維其享年25歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭諾福爾可諾維其。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Nicholas Le Gris于1442年出生在英国諾福爾可布洛迪申时，他的父亲William Le Grys24岁，母亲Margaret Hales20岁。他于1465年与Elizabeth Lapadge在英国英格兰諾福爾可諾維其结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他去世于1467年在英国諾福爾可諾維其享年25岁，埋葬在英国英格兰諾福爾可諾維其。

nameMeanings.name: Le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Gris
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: South German: topographic name for someone who lived in a sandy place, from Middle High German griez ‘sand, pebble’.
Perhaps also a shortened form of German   Griese  .
German: habitational name from any of the places called Gries.

variants.name: Kriss
meanings: Americanized form of an unidentified German surname, probably of   Gries   or   Kress   (compare   Criss  ).
American shortened and altered form of Bulgarian and Macedonian Kristov (see Kristoff ) and of some other related or similarly pronounced Slavic surnames (see Kris ). Compare Criss .

variants.name: Griese
meanings: North German: nickname for a gray-haired man, from Middle Low German grīs ‘gray’.

variants.name: Griess
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Ewald, Kurt, Egon, Heinz, Otto.
German: variant of   Gries  .

variants.name: Creasman
meanings: Posibly also an Americanized form of German Griessmann: topographic name for someone who lived in a sandy place (see Gries ).
Altered form of English   Christman   or German   Christmann  , and possibly also of some other similar (like-sounding) surname (see 2 below). Compare   Crisman  .

variants.name: Greis
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Gunther, Helmut.
German: descriptive nickname for a gray-haired man, from Middle High German grīs ‘gray’.
Altered form of German   Gries  .

variants.name: Criss
meanings: Americanized form of German   Gries   or   Kress  . Compare   Kriss  .
American shortened form of any of various Greek derivatives of the personal name   Christos  . Compare   Chriss  .

American shortened and altered form of Bulgarian or Macedonian Hristov (compare Christoff ) and Kristov (see Kristoff ), and also of some other related or similarly pronounced Slavic surnames (see Kris ). Compare Kriss .

nameMeanings.name: Nicholas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the post-classical Greek personal name Nikolaos, derived from nikē ‘victory’ + laos ‘people’. The spelling with -ch- first occurred as early as the 12th century, and became firmly established at the time of the Reformation, although Nicolas is still occasionally found. St Nicholas was a 4th-century bishop of Myra in Lycia, about whom virtually nothing factual is known, although a vast body of legend grew up around him, and he became the patron saint of Greece and of Russia, as well as of children, sailors, merchants, and pawnbrokers. His feast day is 6 December , and among the many roles which legend has assigned to him is that of bringer of Christmas presents, in the guise of ‘Santa Claus’ (an alteration of the Dutch form of his name, Sinterklaas).

variants.name: Nickolas
meanings: Mainly U.S.: variant spelling of   Nicholas  , influenced by the short form   Nick .

variants.name: Nicolas
meanings: From Late Greek. See Nicholas .

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Nicholas Le Gris was born in 1442, in Brockdish, Norfolk, England, his father, William Le Grys, was 24 and his mother, Margaret Hales, was 20. He married Elizabeth Lapadge in 1465, in Norwich, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died in 1467, in Norwich, Norfolk, England, at the age of 25, and was buried in Norwich, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Nicholas Le Gris est né en 1442, à Brockdish, Norfolk, Angleterre, son père, William Le Grys, avait 24 ans et sa mère, Margaret Hales, avait 20 ans. Il a épousé Elizabeth Lapadge en 1465, à Norwich, Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé en 1467, à Norwich, Norfolk, Angleterre, à l’âge de 25 ans, et a été enterré à Norwich, Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Nicholas Le Gris 1442 in Brockdish, Norfolk, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater William Le Grys 24 und seine Mutter Margaret Hales 20. Er hat 1465 in Norwich, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom Elizabeth Lapadge geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb 1467 im Alter von 25 Jahren in Norwich, Norfolk, England und wurde in Norwich, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Nicholas Le Gris è nato il nel 1442 nel Brockdish, Norfolk, England, il suo padre, William Le Grys, aveva 24 anni e il suo madre, Margaret Hales, aveva 20 anni. Lui sposato Elizabeth Lapadge nel 1465, nel Norwich, Norfolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel 1467, nel Norwich, Norfolk, England, all'età di 25 anni, e fu sepolto a Norwich, Norfolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Nicholas Le Grisが1442年にイギリスノーフォルクブロクディッシュで生まれた時，父William Le Grysは24歳，母Margaret Halesは20歳であった。1465年にイギリスイギリスノーフォルクノーウィチでElizabeth Lapadgeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼は1467年にイギリスノーフォルクノーウィチで25歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスノーフォルクノーウィチにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Nicholas Le Gris(이)가 1442년 영국 노르폴크 브록디시에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William Le Grys(은)는 24세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret Hales(은)는 20세였다. 그는 1465년 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 노위치에서 Elizabeth Lapadge(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1467년 영국 노르폴크 노위치에서 25세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 노위치에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Nicholas Le Gris nasceu em 1442, em Brockdish, Norfolk, Inglaterra, seu pai, William Le Grys, tinha 24 anos e sua mãe, Margaret Hales, tinha 20 anos. Ele casou-se com Elizabeth Lapadge em 1465, em Norwich, Norfolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu em 1467, em Norwich, Norfolk, Inglaterra, com 25 anos, e foi sepultado em Norwich, Norfolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Nicholas Le Gris родился в 1442 г. в местности броккдиш, норфолк, Англия, его отцу по имени William Le Grys было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Margaret Hales, 20. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth Lapadge в 1465 г. в местности норвич, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер в 1467 г. в местности норвич, норфолк, Англия в возрасте 25 лет и был похоронен в местности норвич, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Nicholas Le Gris nació en 1442, en Brockdish, Norfolk, Inglaterra, su papá, William Le Grys, tenía 24 años y su mamá, Margaret Hales, tenía 20 años. Se casó con Elizabeth Lapadge en 1465, en Norwich, Norfolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió en 1467, en Norwich, Norfolk, Inglaterra, a la edad de 25 años, y fue sepultado en Norwich, Norfolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
